expectedDifferences.matcher: regex:^Function definitely-missing-fulfillment-constraint not found\. Ensure that it is released in the current app \([0-9]+\), and that the app is installed\.$
expectedDifferences.reason: The app id interpolated into Shopify's shared functions.function_not_found message is shop/app-specific; compare the stable template and supplied handle while allowing the numeric app id to vary.

expectedDifferences.matcher: regex:^Function gid://shopify/ShopifyFunction/999999999999 not found\. Ensure that it is released in the current app \([0-9]+\), and that the app is installed\.$
expectedDifferences.reason: The app id interpolated into Shopify's shared functions.function_not_found message is shop/app-specific; compare the stable template and supplied function id while allowing the numeric app id to vary.

notes: Live Shopify 2026-04 evidence for fulfillmentConstraintRuleCreate missing identifier, multiple identifiers, empty deliveryMethodTypes, unknown functionId/functionHandle, fulfillmentConstraintRuleUpdate/Delete unknown ids, and the unchanged empty fulfillmentConstraintRules read after those rejected writes. The same capture records that live introspection exposes only fulfillmentConstraintRules, not a singular fulfillmentConstraintRule(id:) root. The current conformance app has no released fulfillment-constraint Function, so success-path, wrong-API-type, and read-after-created-rule live capture require releasing a purchase.fulfillment-constraint-rule.run or cart.fulfillment-constraints.generate.run Function with the required read/write fulfillment constraint scopes.
